summary: Security scan completed. See the findings table below for details.

findings.title: Use of broken cryptographic hashing algorithms for passwords
findings.description: The function hash_password_md5 uses MD5 hashing for passwords which is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collisions and attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use a modern password hashing algorithm such as bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 with proper salt.

findings.title: Use of deprecated SHA-1 hashing for password storage
findings.description: The function hash_password_sha1 uses SHA-1 hashing for password storage which is considered weak and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use modern password hashing schemes like bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 that include salts and are designed for password security.

findings.title: Insecure token storage via base64 encoding
findings.description: The store_token function encodes tokens with base64, which is not encryption and offers no security against disclosure.
findings.recommendation: Encrypt tokens using authenticated encryption algorithms or secure vault mechanisms instead of encoding.

findings.title: Storing plaintext Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in JSON files
findings.description: User sensitive data such as NRIC, date of birth, salary, and bank account are stored in plaintext JSON files without encryption or access control.
findings.recommendation: Encrypt sensitive data at rest and apply strict access controls. Mask or redact PII when stored if possible.

findings.title: Insecure deserialization via pickle.loads on untrusted input
findings.description: Untrusted input is deserialized using pickle.loads, which can lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE) attacks.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using pickle with untrusted data. Use safe serialization formats like JSON or implement strict input validation and sandboxing.

findings.title: TLS certificate verification disabled allowing Man-in-the-Middle attacks
findings.description: TLS certificate verification is disabled with check_hostname set to False and verify_mode set to CERT_NONE, enabling MitM attacks.
findings.recommendation: Always enable and enforce TLS certificate verification to prevent MitM attacks.

findings.title: Use of plain HTTP for sensitive API calls
findings.description: Sensitive API endpoint is accessed over unencrypted HTTP rather than HTTPS, exposing the data to interception.
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS with valid certificates for all sensitive API endpoints.

findings.title: Use of insecure random number generator for security tokens
findings.description: The function uses the non-cryptographically secure random module for generating session tokens.
findings.recommendation: Use the secrets module (e.g., secrets.token_hex) for generating cryptographically secure random tokens.

findings.title: Predictable reset code generation due to fixed random seed
findings.description: The reset code generation seeds the random number generator with a fixed value, resulting in predictable output and insecurity.
findings.recommendation: Do not use a fixed seed. Use cryptographically secure randomness via the secrets module.
